mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear periods with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following INDPRO acceleration, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power during recovery phases defined by GDPC1 growth > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary policy to remain restrictive, crushing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy with a Sharpe ratio difference > 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate profit forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative mean direction in >50% of months with positive payroll growth, or the rolling information coefficient drops below 0.01 over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters meeting the threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, which depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while UNRATE increases
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the predictive accuracy falls below 48% during periods where GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarters.
